# TQUANT

<!-- Generated by scripts/sync_golden_manual.py; do not edit. -->


## Introduction

Quantize an FP32 tile into a lower-precision format (e.g. FP8), producing auxiliary exponent/scaling/max tiles. The quantization mode is a compile-time template parameter (`mode`).

## C++ Intrinsic

C++ declaration:

```cpp
template <auto quant_type, typename TileDataOut, typename TileDataSrc, typename TileDataExp, typename TileDataMax>
PTO_INST void TQUANT(TileDataOut &dst, TileDataSrc &src, TileDataExp *exp, TileDataMax *max, TileDataSrc *scaling);
template <auto quant_type, auto store_mode, typename TileDataOut, typename TileDataSrc, typename TileDataExp,
          typename TileDataMax, typename TileDataIdx>
PTO_INST void TQUANT(TileDataOut &dst, TileDataSrc &src, TileDataExp *exp, TileDataMax *max, TileDataSrc *scaling, TileDataExp *exp_zz, TileDataIdx *vgather_idx);
template <auto quant_type, typename TileDataOut, typename TileDataSrc, typename TileDataPara>
PTO_INST void TQUANT(TileDataOut &dst, TileDataSrc &src, TileDataPara &scale, TileDataPara *offset = nullptr);
```

## Constraints

- This instruction is currently implemented for specific targets.
- Input type requirements and output tile types are mode/target-dependent.

## Math Interpretation

Unless otherwise specified, semantics are defined over the valid region and target-dependent behavior is marked as implementation-defined.

## Assembly Syntax

PTO-AS form: see [PTO-AS Specification](assembly.md).

### AS Level 1 (SSA)

```text
%dst = pto.tquant %src, %qp : (!pto.tile<...>, !pto.tile<...>) -> !pto.tile<...>
```

### AS Level 2 (DPS)

```text
pto.tquant ins(%src, %qp : !pto.tile_buf<...>, !pto.tile_buf<...>) outs(%dst : !pto.tile_buf<...>)
```

### IR Level 1 (SSA)

```text
%dst = pto.tquant %src, %qp : (!pto.tile<...>, !pto.tile<...>) -> !pto.tile<...>
```

### IR Level 2 (DPS)

```text
pto.tquant ins(%src, %qp : !pto.tile_buf<...>, !pto.tile_buf<...>) outs(%dst : !pto.tile_buf<...>)
```
## Examples

See related examples in [this intrinsic manual](index.md) and [the tile-kernel tutorials](../tutorials/tile-kernel.md).

## ASM Form Examples

### Auto Mode

```text
# Auto mode: compiler/runtime-managed placement and scheduling.
%dst = pto.tquant %src, %qp : (!pto.tile<...>, !pto.tile<...>) -> !pto.tile<...>
```


### PTO Assembly Form

```text
%dst = pto.tquant %src, %qp : (!pto.tile<...>, !pto.tile<...>) -> !pto.tile<...>
# AS Level 2 (DPS)
pto.tquant ins(%src, %qp : !pto.tile_buf<...>, !pto.tile_buf<...>) outs(%dst : !pto.tile_buf<...>)
```
